Cambridge Cloud Rally, day win.
If this is climate change, sign me up
Cotswold Regionals #7

Binned 16k from BRO - just couldn't see how I was going to get out again. Just happy to take the 700k! 

It's a 750 on handicap... Or so I will cry to myself
Wheee

Boom boom boom boom

Weird

More weird days at the club class
Cotswolds Regionals #2
The day that kept on giving... fortunately.

I needed a somewhat modest task as I had to present the prizes to the club's trophy winners this evening.
Mostly ballistic, with what felt like a soft patch between Pendock and Mynd but was actually the fastest leg!
Thanks to Andy Davis for showing me how to go fast.

Fairly straightforward, as we were fortunate to avoid the worst of the sea air.
Multi-celled clouds with narrow cores weren't always easy to centre.

A late start, and a struggle to get away, meant that the thermals were dying by the time I came to go back into wind from Edgehill.  Unfortunately the wind was still in full force - if not actually increasing.
Thermals seemed to become more difficult to centre as the day progressed, and Banbury seemed to suck us earthwards each time we passed it.

A flight just for fun.
Sticky getting away and took a while to become confident.  Tricky around the jumble of sea air at Hunstanton.  Then better and better and a storming run home.
Air traffic at Marham, Old Buckenham, Lakenheath and Cambridge all helpful.

Woefully under-declared and under-prepared - no water!  Ballistic into and out of Nympsfield.
What a pity to have to expend so much energy dodging and worrying about airspace.
Patches of incompetence spoiled the speed...
SHOULD HAVE TASKED THE STREETS

The blue early on was not a problem in the east, good cu in the middle of the country until later when the blue marched west and the wind picked up, luckily i was quick enough not to affect me very much getting home.
Almost like a spring day!,
A good not spectacular long day!
Not the promised 7000 ft day!, eventually got to 6000, most thermals early on were very broken.
To many weak climbs under the overcast, when it thinned climbs were 5kts plus, East anglia was dying so turned back at Newmarket
Narrow strip of really good conditions in the middle , west was wave interference and east high cover
Should have declared bigger, but first flight this year, base went to over 4K, with very strong climbs in places, and good streeting earlier.
It felt like we were in a warm sector most of the way, as base was low, vis. was poor, it wasnt until east anglia when the base rose and thermals were a bit more organised.
Sea air
Took a long time to get going, so reduced the task length, the task was near perfect, as we had cu all the way, some tricky spots due to sea air.
A narrow band of good weather down the middle of the country until late on when East Anglia got very good with base of 7K
Poor vis
As expected a bit slow to get going, but very good conditions in East anglia with base almost 5K
Some high cover and some spreadout, but it still worked every time you needed it to, base was 7K at best
Cloud Rally
First XC in 5 years. Further next time.
My first 300 triangle. Must stop faffing when going into wind. Bit soft near Kings Lynn. Great day.
Not my finest, not my fastest, turned back through sheer boredom, but managed to limp home , Andrew flew well and survived my erratic flying !!

Well, another excellent flight ,even with Robert Theil,, surprisingly with few argunents !! The odd 7 knot thermal, but mostly 4 and 5 knots !!
Another special flight considering it's March ..

Another really great day ,good visibility,reliable thermals untill Peterborough then one good climb and home ! Good to have Miles on board, good tracking and good thermalling ,, perfxt tsks setting again by R Theil !

Firstly,many thanks to  the legendary and masterful  task setter ,mr Robert Theil ,who got it just right, again,,,
Fabulous day,lots of 6 and 7 knot thermals, great visibility towards the end, and help from Edgar ,,, good practise day for cloud rally" !!"

Well, that was fun,,,not !!
Managed to make every mistake possible and got away with nothing,,lots of practice in getting away from low, goodness knows how Andrew coped! In truth, very surprised that i got round !
Turned blue on us and 10 k short of bse we turned chicken and headed home ,a fitting end of season flight for two course instructors !!
Fantastic first flight of the season!!

Absolutely wonderful day out ,stunning visibility,7 knot climbs in places, the only down side was that we massively underset !!,,,thanks George for brilliant flying and eysight,  just James and I out today ,was everyone else still in church??

Another great flight with the very companionable Steve Phillips. Visibility not the best but thermals easy to find with many 6 and 7 knots to 4000 feet made the flight fun and relaxing! Thanks to 28 for the perfect task.
Started to feel old and found it even more difficult than usual to centre,abandoned due to having had enough !
Underset,!! Got better and better!
Fantastic day unti i blew it at Kings Lyn,20 miinutes spent grovelling ,,thanks Jonathon for your help!

Come back Mark, all is forgiven ,, 
Great visibility, good flying by Bruno, fantastic task by the incredible task setter Robert T !!
Today is the last time i believe the final glide number,,!! Had to stop at Cambourne at 800 feet and crawl up to glide !!

Cross country instruction for poor Bruno,,terrible visibility until half way round , some good easy times, some downright difficult times,amazed that i had the strength to get round ,,,thanks  Bruno !
Well ,best flight ever...great combination of pilots,7 knot thermals,and fantastic task setting by Robert Theil !!
Really good flight.  Best conditions Andy and Mark have had together.  Doing 105kph, but it went blue at Silverstone (again!), so we slowed down of course.  A great day for both of us !
Excellent day!  Beautiful in the Welsh hills, although tricky on the second leg. Romped home for tea and medals !
Fun flight NE, then north. 316 km
Fantastic flight,  6 and 7 knot thermals. superb visibility, went blue at silverstone so because slightly more interesting! !!
fabulous and easy flight.6 knot thermals and perfect visibility ,,

Set out to grm with high hopes (aren't they always??), got tricky near Silverstone and with little hope of completing the task , we turned Bicester and rattled home with 21 knots up our tail ! Good bit of exercise 💪 for old men, fabulous visibility, everyone 😀 happy !!
350 km jaunt from Gransden Lodge up to bedford bridge, then out into East Anglia. Occasional 7 kt themal and cloud bases around 5000ft. Splendid afternoons activity!!

Fabulous flight, beautiful visibility after Silverstone, a far too interesting final glide,,,,must remember to stay that vital five more minutes in the last thermal to avoid that early aging process !!!
Very difficult but rewarding. Got a bit low at Newmarket otherwise very straightfowards :-).
Had a nice" band of top cover coming with me back from Banbury - could see the edge but it kept moving ahead of me."

ICL at Hus Bos . Late launch, showers across my path; decisions: go to GRL and have the problem of getting back to HB to collect car and trailer, try for HB into 20kt head wind (FES looked like I might(!!) make it) or go for a cup of tea and cake at Sywell? Sywell won with good a/tow back to HB. (Thanks to all at Sywell for their help!)
Personal reasons for the short task - could have done much more!
Another slow trip! I was getting some mad readings from my varios, found that the TE tube was not pushed fully home - possible cause?
Vis was so bad I almost didn't go, but it got better to the west. Original declared task had Brinkley Church as the first tp, but east looked even worse!
Got back earlier than expected, so did a Club 100

That was annoying... having enjoyed my best flight ever, I was keen to see if I could do a 100kph average. Having forgotten my start height I had a search in the computer and somewhere it said Start 3657 feet" (which was the task from the day before... doh!!) so I went for the dive to the line and crossed the airfield going like a ding bat at 600'. 99.55kph was close. It then transpires I started higher and got pinged for a 1098m height loss. Note to self
File created on: make a note of the start height (grrrrr).

Season: Other than that a totally epic day out. "

Notes: Originally declared Six Mile Bottom-Swainsthorpe-Garboldisham-Wymondham-Audley End but binned the GAB bit after a slow start. Grey/blue and soft until Thetford. There was a beautiful puffy line of lift over Lakenheath but the Thursday War" was in full swing so I went around BSE to avoid the F15's and F35's. Thetford lit my afterburner but the epic lift was in a very narrow line hence I binned the "GAB and back" bit. On the way home was set for a straight line to AE but the Lakenheath controller wanted me 5nm away from their airfield as "2 F35's were recovering through 10000'" and needed the airspace hence my tip-toe home in the grey/blue... a slow but lovely day out mind..."
Originally declared 750k. Got delayed starting (grrrr)... turned it into an undeclared 500k as didn't want to land in the dark. My first visit to Talgarth... looks lovely...
Due work in the a.m. started late. Then tactical error on leg 4... should have diverted north or south to get away from the overcast. First flight of the year in the LS6 though :)
The skill available was inversely proportional to the optimism... in my defence I did see a seagull flapping its wings at 3000' whilst thermalling... an odd day...

GRL Cloud Rally Task 7 
GRL-SAC-MOR-BLY-BNW-SWL-NOS-SNS-GRL
Probably the wrong task started at the wrong time... leg 1, 3 and 4 blue... leg 2 mostly blue... and what a laugh trying to get into wind on leg 3 (not!). Good fun though...
Bit of a faff near Wymondham but apart from that a fab flight
What a great day out. Bit weak and mostly blue north of Spalding but otherwise fab for late September…

Missed the fun yesterday so had to fly today albeit with a late start. Didn't know the sky could change so much in 100k. Blue then some cloud, then convergence confusion (just got to GAB in time before the sea air shut it down), then back to blue on the way home. The kink in the climb at 1519 was when a seagull showed me where the proper lift was about 400m west of my position. Oh, and it was hot...

Leg 1 fab 
Leg 2 cloud got lower abeam Norwich and seemed to be building in a random fashion. Norwich ATC super helpful “ if you go below 4000’ it’s a radar service, above it’ll be basic, as you wish”. It was a radar service!
Leg 3 good lift available as it transpired. 
Leg 4 totally blue. “I’ll just steam across this blue bit picking up whopping climbs as I find them”… Er, no you won’t. I found it difficult a) to find lift and b) then stay in it (as SkySight predicted). Final glide was about 7km!! 
Great day out

GRL Cloud Rally Racer Task 6
GRL-CAS-STD-ELM-BKY-BS1-CAS-HDW-GRL
GRL Cloud Rally day 2
That was hard work. I was hoping the thermals would strengthen and have wider cores as the day progressed. If they did, I couldn't find them. As a result, I probably tip-toed too much in between...

Wish I'd gone into East Anglia instead! 
Great day out though... leg 2 went all blue with a funky looking high-level cloud that put Market Harborough in a shadow. As a result, I went all timid.
Leg 3 and 4 fun (downwind of course). Why is it when you want lift you can't find it and when you want to come down (to miss airspace) the thermals go berserk?
Leg 1-2 fun, leg 3 I found it harder to find the good thermals. Leg 4 blue and having deviated north to follow a line of lift then had to go around the Duxford airshow restricted area
GRL Cloud Rally day 1

The forecasts were spot on... a couple of hours of streeting between the morning rain and 15-18 kts aloft in the blue....
Scariest bit was putting the wings back in the trailer!! Should have rigged behind the trees...
A make it up as you go along" day..."
Shakedown. First XC in the new toy...
Very straightforward until I had to go back into wind.
Inter club league. Never knowingly underset. Low and slow, jumping between cut fields. Easiest retrieve ever, thanks Stuart!

Interclub league, day 2. Tricky this side of Northampton. Gorgeous to the west. Took a big detour on the way back to stay in the sun, but the clag turned out to be working fine, so clearly the wrong move!
Cloud Rally Day 1. Pretty good! Just a shame to have dumped the water before the start :(
What a palaver! Cambridge were hugely accommodating though.
Cloud Rally Day 2. Absolutely classic day. Perhaps a little too cautious in places today...
First solo XC - silver distance (and height)
Cloud Rally Day 3. Optimistically loaded up with ballast and hoped for the best, got lucky this time and it seemed to have worked. Would have been a bit faster if I hadn't missed a turnpoint
Task 2 for the day
Junior Nationals Day 3
Shakedown flight after a quiet season for the ICGC Discus. Only one major issue found

Flying the Cloud Rally Racer task non-comp as a warm up for my shift in Team DM tomorrow. Desperately needed some water ballast to make the cruise speeds I had chosen work, dry Peg flies like a brick at 80kts.
Great day out on a long overdue first 300. Got a bit soft at KLY but I knew I had to catch Chris in 869 so kept pushing on. Worked out in the end and I pulled away on glide.
Junior Nationals Day 9
Junior Nationals Day 2

Cloud Rally Funsters Day 4. Went sniffing but had to dump the ballast to stay airborne. Got better after 30mins and I was joined by the rest. Very scratchy on task with no high climbs or strong climbs but managed to make it round.
Junior Nationals Day 8
Wait two years to finish a 300 then do two in two days. Great fun racing the pundits today, not sure i would've set myself a 300 today if there wasnt a race to be had.
Bit of a slog into 20kts. Not surprised by the lack of company on task.
Underset task to start my xc season. Decided it wasn't enough and extended after finishing.
Another challenging flight (thank you Paul Ruskin for an excellent task!)
Probably the last UK XC for me for 2025.  A good note to finish with!
No engine, which was an upset, and then it was a low day working mostly between 2200-3100... Slow, but then parts of it were so overdeveloped and dark I had to take sunglasses off.
A decent speed at last!!!

Intended to try for MEN, but abandoned early.
Went on to try for SPA, but only got just past RAM before returning to GRL.
Enjoyed nearly 4 hours of soaring nevertheless.
My first 300k :)
Another slow attempt, but went further West than before
First jaunt to hus bos and back. Not too slow, for me. Good fun.

Cloud rally day 3, task B
Land out at Little Staughton - doing okay until then!

Grl RUS ALC BOZ PAP SNS (checkpoint)
Cloud rally funster day 7 task A
Club 100km task.
Funster day 1 task A
Turned out to be a challenging day, not what I had expected from rasp. Memorable.
1st cross-country attempt of the season, in windy conditions. Didn't manage to get anywhere near Spalding! Fun flying nevertheless.
Funster day 2 task a
Flew the novice task for the ICL
Silver Distance
Really nice flight ,task was about right. Thanks to Martin (735) the task setter
Good run up to Northampton then it went blue. Turned short at Banbury, that was enough fun for the day!
Short on final glide so landed at Woodchurch Air strip. Friendly welcome and thanks to Colin, Julie and Alan for the retreive
Epic day!
The previous comment wasn't written by me funnily enough. But the flight was a good first mini cross country from AD, even if a very annoying notam stopped my fun.
Adding AATs to the ladder is in fact an absolute pain
Didn't quite manage the 300 but still a pretty awesome flight. I definitely flew a bit too conservatively though, a flight band of 3500-5000ft was stupid.

Mostly nice, but challenging near the northern turn point. Because I lost time on the third leg, it then looked worryingly late for getting home against the wind, but fortunately a diversion north brought some good climbs.

Warm day with 20-25 kph southerly wind. Thermals a little disappointing to start with (3 kts to 3500 ft) given the nice optics. Things improved after 2pm, but it remained a tricky day with plenty of mediocre lift but the occasional good one.
Warm and for a long time claggy at Gransden Lodge. Got going rather late. Conditions improved on the way to Bury St Edmunds, but it remained rather weak and tricky, with fairly low cloud base.

First 750 km flight, and the right day for it. There were some tricky points, in particular at the westerly turnpoints (dumped the water already at the first turnpoint) but there were also some great runs. High cloudbase allowing climbs to 6000ft, moderate westerly wind, and a long day to make good for the time lost by various mistakes.

It turned out to be much better than expected. Blue around the southwesterly turn point, not super-high, moderate climbs, but otherwise pretty nice, so the task  was too small for the day. Best area central East Anglia.

Warm and claggy at Gransden but much better to the west. First turn point at the edge of going blue. Avoided large blue area on second leg by making a diversion to get under nice-looking clouds north of Gloucester. Second turn point area was very good, and there were lots of competition gliders around. Trip home into worsening weather was tricky. Last proper climb at Milton Keynes was just enough to get back. High cover pretty much cut off convection beyond Bedford.

Late start because of a morning commitment, lovely conditions, didn't even go near cloudbase, 6.5k, most of the flight. About 25kph SE, excellent to MTM and down up to Northampton, then blueing out. Home from CAL under whisps, then a bit further to Newmarket in the blue.

Warm but better than the days before. Surprisingly low cloud base at Gransden to start with - and coming down further. Because the base seemed higher towards the east, I set off anyway. Good conditions in East Anglia. Getting back got very tricky from about Thetford because of spreadout. A few drawn-out, slow climbs around Newmarket in the shade gradually got me home.
Big day but chose short flight to get my 100km Certificate Part 2

Cloud Rally Funsters day 2

Great weather for my longest flight yet..
Cloud Rally day 6
Cloud rally winner funsters day 4. But only because Aiden missed the barrels!
Cloud rally

Modest declaration as expecting blue - in the end mostly cu and excellent conditions. However, wasted too much time with turbulent narrow gusty cores on second leg.
Brize confirmed South Cerney not active
Very varied conditions in such a short flight, but very satisfying, good fun and worth every penny of the winch launch cost.
Didn’t find it easy to read the weather today during the flight.
Fabulous conditions, so extended from planned GRM O/R to BUG club task. But sadly lost out on final glide in the blue back into strong wind.
Maybe I should take up another hobby - only added to keep the record of flights for this year - hopefully I will remember how to fly better soon
Cloud Rally
After a romp under cu, blue 60k into Broadway and back to MK. More straightforward for me than other flights this year, just a pain I routed us through the Daventry 4500.

Survival mode for the first half of the flight - cloudbase not high, climbs broken and spread out. Stupidly thought the last 200K would be a romp. Marham very helpful, but not sure going North of Thetford was a good plan as the climbs were weak, although the clouds look OK, and after Diss I ran out of options about 10K away from nice climbs which would have got me closer.

Well that was fun, great that the 2025 season has started.  Best climb 7 knots over 2000' to just short of '6000 near TIB. Great run into NOS, a bit soft coming back past Cambridge, but fabulous again after BSE.

Enjoyed with the usual suspects

Cambridge very accommodating on my way home, allowing me into the ATZ to climb onto glide.

Tricky to get away - good north of Peterborough - less organised after York and struggled for final climb onto glide.
But top day out

Nearly didn't fly today as worried about the wind strength. In the end, not a real issue. Streadout as  we left GRL, then opened up nicely with strong climbs as we went N.  The advantage of the strong wind was that Sibson was inactive, so cleared to go through. Past Grantham, less sun on the ground and a bit gloomy, so was cautious in, and diverted well W on the way out of Newark. Strange blue hole from Rutland back to Alconbury, but then booming on the run in.

Sometimes the most enjoyable flights are not the fastest or longest.

It took many years to do my first 750, but 7 years on and given the forecast it seemed rude not to join the Gransden 750 trip out.  Mostly fabulous conditions, with a lot of airspace watching, but the odd moment to keep me on my toes, especially the second trip West when high cover made the climbs a bit soft.
But, first normal thermal day I have flown on this year and thankfully I seem to have broken the curse of avgas burning.

Well done to all those who achieved badge flights today - lots of them!
Started too early, and clearly underset, but a lovely day out and all for the cost of a winch launch !

Not the outcome I wanted, but nice to have my first trip over the Peaks and around Birmingham.
Too slow and ran out of options and day.
Well done to all those who did better
Don't regret trying - another time!

That was fun !
Strong thermals, good streeting, great vis.
Lovely trip to the seaside - photos to follow
Great fun - strong climbs and high cloudbase - lovely sight seeing flight and today the blue slog back home into wind was not as bad as yesterday!

Very murky heading out - on the run from Silverstone to Andover, great climbs, when you could find them, and a lot of mid level spread out in between which caused me to be too cautious. After digging out of a small hole post Andover, good run back. Ok into Wisbech, but things starting to switch off.
Great task setting by Robert
We were not in the best part of the country today - nice afternoon jaunt once we got away, great West of the M1
ICL and I was not very good!
Went via Brigg to try and catch the sea-breeze-front. It didn’t work so I struggled northbound. Southbound the glider seemed to know where to go……

Task GRL-BCW-WHN-NOW-GRL was declared, but the calculator didn't use the correct distance, so I’ll mark it as undeclared.

Conditions west of Banbury were becoming increasingly soft and difficult. I dumped water near BCW, then spent some time patiently waiting for a climb that would allow me to comfortably get over Cleeve Hill.

Conditions improved east of Banbury, with strong climbs mixed with areas of heavy sink. I didn’t want to land too late, so I decided to head back to GRL.

Obviously, I would have loved to complete the task, but it was a rewarding challenge to climb away from such a low point in tricky conditions north of Cheltenham.

The weather turned out better than what Skysight had predicted. I just followed one of the suggested tasks from Skysight. In hindsight, it might have been smarter to stay further south, since there was thick cloud cover (topcover) over the northern part of the route.

The first leg had some cumulus clouds, which helped. But the second leg was mostly blue sky with thick topcover near Leicester, making it harder to find lift. Luckily, there were still some thermals around. The first part of the leg to Hardwick had no clouds at all. I found a few climbs downwind of towns and other good trigger points, but at one point I dropped below 2000 ft above ground level and really needed a thermal.

The last climb, just north of Grafham Water, was the strongest and got me the highest. I’m happy I managed to finish the task.
Good energy lines!

A tricky start, but had a good first climb near Kettering. Hit a big blue hole north of Newark. The return leg south was more straightforward. The sky looked ominous south of Rutland from top cover, but I got lucky with a solid climb after the last TP at Corby, which set me up for a comfortable final glide—though there was no real lift on the final glide.

The first leg was mostly between 2000–3000 ft. Marham being closed until 12:45 LT helped, and on the return, they let me use all their airspace. Interestingly, the into-wind legs felt easier than the tailwind ones. After Bedford, I couldn’t find any lift under many clouds and nearly had to land back at Gransden. Luckily, an ASK21 marked a climb that got me back to cloudbase. Near Halesworth, a sea breeze front provided a strong climb and a dramatic view next to a tall wall of cloud. The final leg was fairly straightforward, though it was clear the day was ending.
First flight of the year. Quite some streeting but not always easy to find the climbs. Finished a bit higher to have another engine test run.

My first 600km FAI triangle flight. I found it tricky on the first leg with some overdevelopment and spread-out clouds. Second leg started off the same but later on most clouds were working. That was helpful navigating between all the airspace. Started to get pretty good on thew way to Pencombe. Last leg I had a struggle to find a good climb but after I got a climb to 4000ft plus the climbs were quite strong and reliable.
Tricky at the beginning and end of flight. Occasional high amount of sink in the blue on final glide. Although overall better climbs were had in the blue.

I had trouble finding good lift on the way to the first waypoints although the clouds looked good. Was kind of blue near Stickney Bridge/tp1. The cloud base was around 4,500–5,000 ft, but most thermals stopped working at about 3,000–3,500 ft. After the first turnpoint flying south, the lift south of Peterborough was much better. There were also some great cloud streets although they were 30 degrees off course.

As usual I was one of the last to launch, which probably made the last leg slower and more difficult than it could have been. 2000' feels ridiculously low when you've spent most of the task above 5000'!
Congratualations to Lauri for completing the task that I set and abandoned.
First XC of the season, and surely the longest by far that I've ever flown in April. Awesome climbs mid-afternoon, but none to be found in the last 40k. Thanks to Wenshu for setting a fun task.
Pleasant stress-free flight at an age appropriate speed.
GPS failed on last leg.  It thinks date is in 2006 due to not handling 1024 week wraparound.  Apart from that an excellent day out.
An upwind meander with upper wind varying 20 to 25 kts. Thermals (and sink) got stronger as the flight progressed.  A pleasant way to pass the day.

The smoke from the upwind fire on an industrial estate in Rushden cast a pall over the club with poor conditions overhead all morning so I took a tow towards Potton and did a remote start at the Potton mast.  It worked.  The rest of the flight was great as long as you stayed high (above about 4000’).  Had a final glide home from between Saltby and Belvoir Castle – best part of 90 km.
Pootled around a 200k, with the aim of getting my 5hrs, which I just about managed! I didnt have a final glide, to remain at cloudbase to long out the flight, so it probably knocked some speed off.
1st solo XC of the year, horrendously slow, but good fun. Strong climbs, but in strange places under the cumulus due to no wind.
Day 1 of the Cambridge GC cloud rally
Day 2 of the East Midlands ICL at Gransden.
Day 2 of CGC cloud rally. Wow! Much better than expected, should of flown a bit faster!
Day 2 of the Junior Nationals, blue up to 3500'
Final day of the Junior Nationals.
Great leg into & out of NPT, but got harder past Cambridge & got stuck 10k West of BSE, then got rained on twice!
Difficult pushing into a 25kt wind, 7/8ths at the start, first 1/2 of the leg took a massive chunk off the speed. Conditions really improved after a few hours, I should of launched an hour later!
Junior Nationals day 6
Andy thought this task would be the only way to beat Rob Theil.. almost made it to Edgehill, but had to turn back 20k short due to rain! Good fun anyway.
Bit of a distaster before the task, fell out the winch launch, waited for the tug to refuel, then pulled off in a rubbish thermal!! Almost landed out 8km from home but managed to scrape home.
Cross-country training for James with a focus on streets. :-)

Cross-country training with James Barton-Williams. OK as far as Peterborough on the return, but progressively more difficult as the sky became more claggy and the headwind strengthened. We stayed mostly in range of some alternate airfields but JBW saved the day with some well-found climbs.
Faffed at the start and end, but the eastern triangle was very nice.
Reasonably fast to Great Malvern in strong conditions then very slow coming back in the blue.

Cross-country training with James Barton-Williams who is annoyingly good at thermalling. :-) 
Excellent conditions which allowed us to airfield hop which was required due to the lack of a trailer. :-o
Kai's first cross-country!

On the whole a relaxed flight. Despite being warm and humid the cloudbase was high and there was some streeting. It became a little tricky on the last leg as the sky turned milky, the wind turned through 90 degrees and decent climbs became harder to find. Some lovely views of the north Norfolk coast.
Wild at times.
The Perkoz does not go as well into wind as a Ventus! Luckily Faustyna was a great help on her first cross-country.

Always fun to fly around the Birmingham airspace.
First leg good apart from a low point (overconfidence?) that cost some time then and also probably later. Cloudbase already 6500' at Sheffield. Tiptoed under the 5500' across the Peaks which were looking lovely in the sun. Once finally through I was amazed to find cloudbase was now 7500' and matched by the occasional 7.5 knots. Across the wind it was all a bit climb and glide, but after Hay I was finally able to string some stuff together albeit somewhat off track. Getting down to 5000'? Need a climb! 

Once again it turned into a struggle in the blue grinding away to stay airborne. After a few anxious moments mentally rehearsing the engine start sequence and picking backup fields near Milton Keynes, I finally got a couple of climbs at Olney windfarm and Bedford that got me home. A little lift on final glide allowed a high speed finish. Glad to get round but should have been a bit faster.
The competition director wisely decided to scrub this task. Epic struggle with 841.

Hard work as far as Oxford, where I got low and almost decided to abandon the task. But after finding a climb, I could see a couple of good clouds on track and decided to press on a bit further.  Things got much better from there and all was going well until I got to Oxford on the way back, where I had to scrape around at 2000ft in less than a knot for what seemed like ages. When I did eventually find a good climb, the rest was easy.
First leg was much harder than expected. 2nd Leg (160km into wind) was just as hard as expected! Then tip-toed back to Gransden at best glide. A challenging day, but very rewarding.
Tricky
A nice day with some good climbs and thermals when needed. Blue in the East, but OK.
A day of highs and lows!  Very slow (again) but I got round...
What a super day...

Delayed getting away. Then failed to get away from aw inch launch.  Downgraded the task twice before finally launching by aerotow, but seem to have got it right in the end.
Slow first leg into wind, not helped by getting stuck at 1,300 for 10 min when everything seemed to stop working. But turned Edghill in a nice thermal to 5,000 ft.
The return leg looked horrid, but in fact there were some good climbs under the embedded cumulus, and I was on final glide after Silverstone.  Tiptoed home at 5.15pm under the overcast.
Fun.

Everything seemed to work out well, except the sky ahead heading for the final turn point looked very dodgy, and I was seriously thinking of abandoning and heading for home.  But I pressed on to what seemed like the last decent cloud, and by the time I got there, another one had appeared ahead, and this continued until it was too late to abandon.  Nice gentle glide home downwind at best glide got me home with height to spare
First cross country for a while. Good fun.
Cloud rally.  Day 1

Cloud Rally Day 2.
A slow patch at Edgehill but very good all the rest of the way.
Final glide got better and better from 300ft under at Rushden to 1500ft arrival.
Spot the navigational error on second leg.
I got the memo and took the day off.  It was well worth it!

Of course it was declared, I even declared it on WeGlide(where it apears as complete, of course) but it seems that the fantastic" Oudie N decided to keep the declaration from the last flight. Shame as it was a really nice day
File created on: very slow starting in the blue but towards the North it was quite good."

Notes: The fantastic" Oudie N doesn't calculate the 1000m difference between start and finish altitudes so I crossed there finish line with over 2000 ft since I couldn't remember the start altitude... There's 2 kph right there..."
Cross country in the Junior...my sole ambition was not to land out. :)
Blue and slow...
My computer said 86km/h the ladder seems to have a different idea. Maybe height loss...
My computer said 94km/h but probably the difference was due to height loss > 1000m... Anyway good fun, 100km/h was easily possible... Maybe next time

First day of a task week at Lleweni Parc run by Chris Gill. As the forecast weather was iffy" Chris set a free distance task with up to 6 legs scored on WeGlide. We clocked up 247 km like that. There was a fantastic convergence running up and down the coast from Prestatyn to Conwy. The last runs up and down it were over the sea
File created on: sadly no photos of this. On the second run up the convergence we crossed onto the westerly side of Snowdonia and picked our way to just short of Dinorwig where my nerve ran out.  Later in the day to the west of Ruthin it started waving between 2 and 4 knots to 8000ft. A grand day out."

Notes: All going jolly well till Methwold. At least I managed not to land out 😁

All going jolly well till around Harrogate. The wind was westerly about 15knots and the hills were chopping up the thermals. We got within 7km of Leyburn but just couldn't make the last bit safely. Setting of back from Masham at 4pm with about 250km to go felt challenging to put it mildly. Really rather surprised to get back without using the engine. Thanks to Phil for some excellent flying and putting up with my frustration.

Another free distance task day at Lleweni Park. Declared a task that looked appropriate for the forecast. Conditions were much worse to the south than forecast sooner than forecast so abandoned and clocked up 265km free distance including turning Snowdon. A grand day out
Lovely flight with Phil O'Keefe. Ended up formation flying with his son Alex O'Keefe in his Nimbus 2
Oh so slow.. First time round Birmingham
Task aligned well with the streets

A low initial cloudbase made me pretty cautious, and I never really changed gears. The thermals were very reliable: sunny side of the cloud - boom. Closer to Newark the cloudbase went up and the run to Kettering was easy. But by the time I got close to GRL the topcover came in and it all went a bit soft. I decided to cut my losses and head home.
Booming. Mostly blue after Calvert. Got myself into the weed after Bedford and had to dig myself out again :(

I'm glad I flew. It looked pretty murky and depressing to begin with, but things got better towards the west. The run back East was easy with the wind at my back and good streeting. Things looked a bit dire after GRL towards the East, but I kept going and Bury came up quickly. The run back from Bury initially looked promising with some streeting, but I got myself into a hole after a Newmarket and spent an age getting on glide. But, as I said, I'm glad I flew!
I found it pretty hard work with a low cloudbase and hard to centre climbs. No real low points, but happy to make it round after a landout last time out.
A nice day out! Leg to Methwold was a bit iffy, but worked out ok in the end. Thanks to 28 for the inspiration for the task.
First XC of the year. A few stonking thermals on the way East. Got a bit soft on the way back. Thanks for the task Phil!
First 500km 🥳
It was all going great until we turned Eastwell and it was all blue to the East. Put of by the yesteday's landout I decided to call it a day and pootle my way home.

Excellent thermals with strong high climbs.   High cloudbase meant that Daventry airspace was much more of a nuisance than expected - should have put a checkpoint in on the way back to route round it!  Last leg was blue, soft and into wind but started with a lot of height.  Third day I've had flying the LS6 so happy with this.
Tricky getting out of GRL but lots of good air afterwards.  A little bit in the middle where I struggled a bit.

A bit tricky going into Radway, where the sky wasn't very well organised and becoming over developed.  A little rain on the way out, but then into really good air with high climbs when eventually I connected.  Ran a line of Cu against the sea air between Wisbech and Moulton Church, then sea air all the way back to about 5km from GRL.
I thought it was under-declared, but with the top cover killing the day on the way home it turned out just right.
Rather constrained by the weather and the RA(T)s.  But worked reasonably well.  Climbs very narrow again.

This started off as GRA GRM with Mike Smith.  But it was blue after Cambridge and after turning Great Ashfield I pushed too hard and a couple of hot spots that should have worked didn't, and I had to use the engine.

So I went back through Gransden and flew GRL, BUG, a club task.  This was very fast on the way out, but as forecast, conditions deteriorated on the way back after Banbury and good climbs were hard to find.
Harder than the sky looked!
Was meant to be MEM, MET, DIS.  First leg and a half really difficult with lots of spreadout and consequently slow.  Decided at MET to go home to dinner.  Challenging.
I think we made the most of the weather.

Good task (thanks 900) - and strong climbs throughout. Only tricky bit was on the final glide where I was a little below what I needed and the rather scrappy cu I diverted to didn't provide the necessary. Had to stop and top up in a weak blue thermal 10k from GRL.

Windy, and Gransden became seriously overcast as we got airborne, as did Newark when we got there.  In between, had to navigate the blue air from the Wash.  However, the air mass was quite good and there were some good climbs in there.  Perhaps not the best task, but good fun.
Nice flight for the first set task of the season
Poor visibility for the first half, but much better after Earith.
Nice task with some lovely views.  Slower than the forecast suggested, but timed it right to get out for dinner in the evening!
The weather was a lot better than Skysight had claimed.  Albeit quite a lot of the task was in the blue.
Fairly straightforward.  Blue but working well to Banbury, and from Towcester on the way home.  Reasonable Cu for the rest.  Some good climbs.
This was also my silver distance flight!
Used a turning point that wasn't a turning point - that was today's lesson!

Forecast was poor so did not declare a task. Big mistake as conditions were really quite good. First flight in M8 this year so really a shake down flight. Used the engine for 50 seconds after after getting away so had to use St Neots as a remote start. Great fun.
Difficult to get away from GRL. Late start. Needed to back to GRL by 16.30hrs so turned back.
A super flight
Some good bits and some soft bits. Had a head wind on first 2 legs which didn't help.

Another difficult day. Only a handful of decent round and solid thermals. Most very broken. Controllers as always helpful. Wittering approved entry to their ATZ but fortunately I didn't need it. Pleased to get round the task. Thanks Robert (909) for the task.
A super flight. shared a few thermal with 841 and 871. Just manage to derig before we had heavy rain.

Some scruffy cu early on then mainly blue with high cover. The route ahead always looked awful but reasonable climbs some of the time with some soft areas. Had a struggle north of Oxford A/F. Controller very helpful. Pleased to get round.

Declared Broadway-Newmarket. High cover on way back from Broadway impacted thermal strength so had to take a diversion to the north to stay with some sun. Hoped to be able to get to Newmarket but too much high cover. Just pleased to get back to GRL

A much better day than expected. A couple of soft areas otherwise a super flight. Thanks to LIZ who marked a thermal during a soft patch after turning Methwold where I got quite low.. Thanks also to Paul (28) for another good task.
Thanks Paul (28) for the task. Went round the last turn point lower than ideal and struggled to find good climbs for a while due to spread-out. A fun day.
A good day for mid September. Conditions went soft east of Cambridge.
Streets didn't align with my task. Also, no water an error. Despite that it was a fun flight

The first leg was blue after the 20-30kms, but worked well until GRA when it was rather soft. Cu after about St Neots then a good run to GRM. Blue again from Banbury and a mix of weak and good climbs into a headwind. Pleased to get round.
Mostly blue at the start. Good climbs on first and second legs. Didn't find a decent climb until Ely on last leg. Thanks Paul (28) and Robert W (W8) for the task
A good run to Fakenham. After that climbs variable as it was rather soft in places. Shared several thermals with W8 and 700. Thanks Wendy (700) and Robert (909) for the task.
Started early to avoid the Cloud Rally gaggles. This was a bad decision as it was soft around BAN/EDG. Otherwise a fun task.

A challenge all the way round. Low cloud base and spread out on first. Second leg conditions did improve north of HUS. Many thermals broken. Weakening conditions on last leg. Wasn't convinced task was achievable until onto final glide. Pleased to have got round even if it was a tough day. Thanks Wendy (700) for the task.
Lots of local thermalling at the end to try to build hours - I should have gone to the finish point first, but all this is new to me!
Should probably have been more ambitious but got a late start! Declared to Simon Smith
Sightseeing tour of Cambridge then footling around in thermals to get my 5 hour silver duration

morning work so afternoon task
survival mode from A1 to M11 due to falling out of the best convection !
lost too much time for BSE to be a sensible option
mostly a struggle but shout out to Marham for a clearance to enter their DA if required
A big thank you to Jem for lending me his esteemed flying machine while he is on holiday !!

Weird start at GRL due warehouse fire at Rushden (almost unsoarable overhead for several hours)
outbound leg 3 hour nightmare
inbound leg 1hr 15 mins of a dream sky if you stayed high

More of a struggle than  expected !! My vario averager seemed stuck on 3 point something most of  the  day whatever I did. High enough to photograph the lake this time but not the sea air incursion 909 warned me about from Hereford to Evesham !  Should have followed the gang going the other way round !
Nice day out with the usual suspects.  I think we flew in the best area available.
Task set by 28.  A weak start didn’t inspire confidence but it got better in places.

Couldn't make it work in the hills on the 2nd leg so a bit slow.  But the first time around Birmingham :-)
Thanks to 700 for the task.

Slow to the first tp but the weather switched on properly thereafter.  Glorious in East Anglia with high cloud base.  Some spread out at the last tp but then a good run home.  Congratulations to all who did their first 300, 500 and 750K flights.
A difficult flight, weather not as forecast.
Excellent task set by 28 and 700.  Late start but got back just before the spread out.
A very good start with the last 100K becoming difficult with broken thermals.  Task setter 700 got it right again.
Warm up flight for the cloud rally.  Much better than anticipated.
Another difficult flight with very few round thermals but I cant blame anybody else for poor task setting.  Glad to complete."
A short flight to finish the season.  Flying dry was a mistake.
A good day out.  First 2 legs slow but the last 200K were almost like the forecast.  A turbulent day.
First x-c in my new toy.  The forecast didn't show much promise but conditions were reasonable to Bicester and excellent in East Anglia.
Turbulent again and broken thermals but mainly good conditions.  What a great season.
Another well set task by Paul Ruskin.  A longer and better day than anticipated.
Landed at Little Staughton as my final glide was marginal.
Another good task with the GRL crowd.  Poor visibility and spread out for the first 250K but then the sun came out and made life much easier.
Probably the best day I've flown on.  A privilege to be up there in a glider.  Thanks to 700 for the task.
Got stuck for a bit near St Neots.  Apart from that straight forward although good climbs were difficult to find.
Apart from a late start and sea air penetration on the way into East Anglia a good day out.
A flight of 2 halves, nothing like the forecast until Belvoir, excellent in East Anglia.  A good day out with 205 and 871.
The best day I have flown from GRL in a SE'ly wind.
Soft and unpredictable until 20K short of the 2nd turn point when condition became excellent.
For once I resisted changing my task and tried to fly this trophy task fast.  A bit blue at the first TP and blue again from Milton Keynes.
Good task set by Wendy 700.  Left to early and then had to dig myself out of a hole at the last turn point.
Tricky to get away from Gransden but from Peterborough onwards it was excellent with a high cloud base.
Late start and evening appointment, so shorter than I would have liked.
Wave flight on a CUGC expedition to Lleweni Parc.
Wave flight on CUGC expedition to Lleweni Parc.
Strange looking sky all day, but good climbs around. Pleased to get home when I did as top cover was beginning to shut down the convection.
Good romp until Banbury on the way home - blue and difficult. Started too late.
Odd day with occasional strong stable climbs. The final leg from Methwold offered more of a challenge.

Thanks to Paul Ruskin for the task. I anticipated that the task would be affected by sea air. As it happens IPE was clear, but the sea breeze front was at the Halesworth. Soft 1st leg to BED, but improving conditions going into East Anglia. Easy final leg home with 6kts to get onto glide.

I was optimistic that the day would last long enough to complete 300km. As it happened the conditions switched off as rapidly as they switchen on. I was in a climb at TOW that would have got on to final glide for GRL, but was still in XC mode to NMT so left climb early and encountered no more lift :-(
Good day for late September. A few blue holes and difficiult 20km in/out of Ely due to sea air.
Wrong task for the day. Should have tasked along the wind direction. Hardwork around Eastwell - lost the will to continue.
Grim 1st leg - better southbound.
Excellent run out to NOS, but conditions were softer from GRW to beyond Mildenhall. 8kt climb after TIB - probally sea breeze convergence. Spread out on the way home.

Great flight :-) Arrived at Lake Vyrnwy just before 2pm (in 2 hrs 06 mins) I considered continuing to Snowdon (50km further), but was very conscious of issues with the blue and sea incursion the day before from SIL onwards making the final 70km difficult. As it happened the blue was better today, but still killed the speed. However, if I'd turned SWD it would have been more difficult an hour later.
Good fun task. A bit soft around Crowland otherwise ok. I have could done with some water!

1st XC flight this year. 
Good conditions in East Anglia with top cover to the West etc. I added Bedford Bridge O/R as it was too early to land :-).

Difficult at Gransden - with gliders landing back.  It took an hour to get to get to a sensible start height. First climb at Alconbury then good conditions with long glides and some very good climbs. Expected it to be difficult getting back, but it wasn't.
Thanks to Wendy for the task. Started too early (unusual for me) and got low a couple of times that cost time.
For whatever reason this task didn't flow" - hence speed. However
First 2 legs were fine, but started too late (again). 60km to NMT and home were difficult as blue conditions were closing down. Grateful to get home.

The sky never looked very enticing. Difficult second leg; good leg to Wisbech, but conditions weakening due to top cover.
Second attempt at task by road to retrieve Mike S (M8) from Andover was somewhat slower :-(
First 500k! :) Not easy in the east, and had to dodge airspace in the west. Otherwise great fun and I'm so glad I've done it.

The sky was full of energy - gusty, turbulent, but luckily the thermals were reliable despite the wind and not broken up. Great energy line between Soham and Eastwell and high cloud base all around (7000'). Had to drop below 5000' to avoid the airspace after Calvert Junction where it also turned blue. With a strong headwind going home, it definitely made me feel uncomfortable. But thanks for the great tips from the pundits, I managed to find a few strong climbs just downwind of hot spots. Glad to make it home!
First time to King's Lynn. The first leg was great but things got slow towards Crowland and afterwards. Glad to make it home!
A hot and difficult day...Original task going to Edgehill and Ramsey after Littleport but the thermals had been unreliable and difficult to centre, so decided to abort near Northampton and go home.
cloud rally day 2

Not too bad for April! It was fun but also stressful at places, as I thought nil wind should make thermals quite easy to spot but it wasn't quite the case as some clouds just didn't deliver. After turning Rattlesden, the sky was deteriorating and there weren't much going on past Cambridge. Luckily a high climb to 6000' got me to a final glide 40 km away.
First gold height. Well, not the diamond height I was aiming for due to too many other contributing factors  in the day. Will try it another day.
